name: GE 7FA Gas Turbine Stage 1 Blade (Set of 92)
description: The GE 7FA Stage 1 turbine blade set consists of 92 directionally-solidified superalloy blades for the first-stage rotating assembly. Coated with advanced thermal barrier coating (TBC), these blades withstand inlet temperatures up to 1,370°C in heavy-duty gas turbines.
shortDescription: Stage 1 rotating blade set (92 pcs), DS superalloy, TBC coated, for GE 7FA.
technicalDescription: Directionally solidified (DS) GTD-444 superalloy blades with internal serpentine cooling passages, showerhead film cooling, and MCrAlY bond coat with YSZ thermal barrier coating. Each blade serialized with full material traceability.
features: Directionally solidified superalloy for high-temperature creep resistance
Internal serpentine cooling with film cooling holes
Full material traceability with individual serialization
benefits: Extends hot gas path inspection intervals
Improves turbine efficiency with optimized aerodynamics
Reduces lifecycle cost with increased durability
weight: 0.85 kg per blade (78 kg per set)

downloads.name: GE Turbine Hot Gas Path Brochure

seoTitle: GE 7FA Gas Turbine Stage 1 Blade Set
seoDescription: GE 7FA stage 1 turbine blade set (92 pcs) made of DS GTD-444 superalloy with TBC coating. For power generation and combined cycle gas turbines.

faq.question: What is the expected service life?
faq.answer: Typical service life is 24,000-36,000 operating hours before first inspection, depending on operating conditions and fuel type.

faq.question: Can these blades be repaired?
faq.answer: Yes, GE-authorized repair shops can perform tip restoration, coating renewal, and cooling hole reclamation.

faq.question: What fuel types are compatible?
faq.answer: Compatible with natural gas, distillate oil, and crude oil. Heavy fuels may reduce interval life.

name: Siemens SGT-750 Stage 1 Nozzle Ring
description: The Siemens SGT-750 stage 1 nozzle ring directs hot combustion gases onto the first-stage turbine blades. Each 24-vane segment set is investment cast from Inconel 738 LC with internal cooling and TBC coating.
shortDescription: Stage 1 nozzle ring assembly, 24 vanes, Inconel 738 LC, TBC coated for SGT-750.
technicalDescription: Stage 1 stationary nozzle ring in 6 four-vane segments. Investment cast IN-738 LC superalloy with internal impingement cooling, showerhead film cooling on trailing edge, and MCrAlY + YSZ TBC. Full dimensional and material certification.
features: Advanced MCrAlY + YSZ thermal barrier coating
benefits: Optimized flow path improves stage efficiency
Reduced cooling air requirement improves cycle efficiency
Extended inspection interval with durable TBC

downloads.name: Siemens SGT-750 Hot Gas Path Manual

seoDescription: Siemens SGT-750 stage 1 nozzle ring, 24-vane set, Inconel 738 LC, TBC coated. For power generation and mechanical drive gas turbines.

faq.question: What is the typical service interval?
faq.answer: The nozzle ring typically requires inspection at 24,000 hours with major overhaul at 48,000 hours.

faq.question: Can individual segments be replaced?
faq.answer: Yes, individual 4-vane segments can be replaced without replacing the entire ring, reducing maintenance costs.

faq.question: What is the material of the TBC?
faq.answer: The thermal barrier coating is yttria-stabilized zirconia (YSZ) applied over an MCrAlY bond coat.

name: Rolls-Royce Trent Combustion Liner Assembly
description: The Rolls-Royce Trent combustion liner is a key component of the annular combustion chamber. Fabricated from high-temperature superalloy sheet metal with effusion cooling, this liner ensures complete fuel combustion and minimal emissions in Trent gas turbine engines.
shortDescription: Combustor liner assembly for Rolls-Royce Trent, effusion cooled, CMC tiles.
technicalDescription: Annular combustion chamber liner with effusion cooling holes, ceramic matrix composite (CMC) tiles at hot spots, and internal heat shield assembly. Full ring fabricated from Haynes 230 alloy with thermal barrier coating.
features: Effusion cooling for uniform temperature distribution
Full ring construction with expansion joints
benefits: Reduces emissions to meet environmental regulations
Extends hot section life with advanced cooling
Lightweight design improves power-to-weight ratio
seoDescription: Rolls-Royce Trent annular combustion liner with effusion cooling, CMC tiles, Haynes 230 alloy. For aero-derivative gas turbine power generation.

faq.question: What is effusion cooling?
faq.answer: Effusion cooling uses thousands of tiny laser-drilled holes to create a cool air film over the liner surface, reducing metal temperatures.

faq.question: Are CMC tiles replaceable?
faq.answer: Yes, individual CMC tiles can be replaced during shop visits, extending the life of the base liner.

faq.question: What fuel types can this combustor burn?
faq.answer: Designed for natural gas with liquid fuel backup. Dual-fuel (gas and liquid) configurations are available.

name: GE 7FA Turbine Journal Bearing Assembly
description: The GE 7FA turbine journal bearing assembly supports the turbine rotor shaft at the exhaust end. This tilting-pad bearing features five shoes with babbit lining, a hardened steel shaft journal, and flood lubrication design.
shortDescription: Tilting-pad journal bearing, 5-shoe, babbit lined, for GE 7FA turbine.
technicalDescription: 5-pad tilting pad journal bearing with steel-backed babbit lining, flood lubrication, and integral temperature sensors. Precision-bored to OEM clearances. Includes oil drain, feed ports, and thermocouple provisions.
features: 5-pad tilting pad design for shaft stability
benefits: Prevents rotor dynamic instability at high speeds
Reduces vibration with optimum clearance control
Extends bearing life with proper lubrication
material: Steel Body with Tin Babbitt Lining
seoDescription: GE 7FA tilting-pad journal bearing assembly for turbine rotor support. 5-shoe, babbit lined, flood lubrication. OEM specifications.

faq.question: What is the recommended oil flow?
faq.answer: Recommended oil flow is 120 L/min at 1.5 bar supply pressure with ISO VG 32 turbine oil.

faq.question: What temperature alarms are included?
faq.answer: Each pad has an embedded thermocouple. Alarm is typically set at 100°C with trip at 115°C.

faq.question: Can this bearing be rebuilt?
faq.answer: Yes, babbit re-pouring and re-machining is possible within OEM limits, typically up to 2 times.

name: Siemens SGT-600 Fuel Injector (Dry Low Emissions)
description: The Siemens SGT-600 dry low emissions fuel injector is a premix burner that reduces NOx emissions by thoroughly mixing fuel and air before combustion. The multi-nozzle design achieves single-digit NOx levels on natural gas.
shortDescription: Dry low emissions fuel injector for SGT-600, premix design, NOx < 9 ppm.
technicalDescription: Dual-fuel (gas/liquid) premix injector with swirl-stabilized flame. Pilot and main fuel stages for turndown operation. Hastelloy X construction with effusion-cooled burner tip. Includes flame detector port and spark plug access.
features: Dry low emissions premix combustion design
benefits: Achieves single-digit NOx emissions (< 9 ppm)
Stable combustion across wide load range
Fuel flexibility with natural gas and liquid fuel
seoDescription: Siemens SGT-600 dry low emissions fuel injector, premix design, < 9 ppm NOx, Hastelloy X. For industrial gas turbine combustion systems.

faq.question: What is the service interval for this injector?
faq.answer: Recommended inspection interval is 8,000 hours with replacement typically at 24,000 hours.

faq.question: Can this be retrofitted on older turbines?
faq.answer: Yes, the injector is designed for retrofit of SGT-600 turbines with standard combustion systems to DLE.

faq.question: What liquid fuels are compatible?
faq.answer: Compatible with diesel, light crude, and kerosene. Heavy fuel oils require a different injector variant.

name: GE 7FA Interstage Seal Ring
description: The GE 7FA interstage seal ring minimizes leakage between turbine stages, maintaining aerodynamic efficiency and cooling flow integrity. The honeycomb abradable seal design allows tight clearances with rub-tolerant operation during start-up.
shortDescription: Interstage honeycomb seal ring for GE 7FA turbine, reduces leakage.
technicalDescription: Segmented seal ring with honeycomb abradable seal material (hastelloy honeycomb), steel backing ring, and spring-loaded segments for radial compliance. Designed to fit between stator diaphragm and rotor platform.
features: Honeycomb abradable seal for minimal running clearance
Segmented design for thermal expansion accommodation
Hastelloy honeycomb material for high-temperature durability
benefits: Improves turbine efficiency by reducing interstage leakage
Minimizes blade tip clearance loss over operating cycle
Increases power output by maintaining aerodynamic efficiency
seoDescription: GE 7FA interstage seal ring with honeycomb abradable material. 8-segment design for leakage control in gas turbine hot gas path.

faq.question: What is an abradable seal?
faq.answer: An abradable seal has a soft, rub-tolerant surface that allows rotating parts to cut their own groove during initial operation, minimizing running clearance.

faq.question: What is the replacement interval?
faq.answer: Typically replaced during major hot gas path inspections at 48,000 hour intervals.

faq.question: Can the honeycomb be repaired?
faq.answer: Minor damage can be repaired. Extensive wear or crushing requires replacement of individual segments.

name: Rolls-Royce Trent Turbine Discharge Nozzle
description: The Rolls-Royce Trent turbine exhaust diffuser collects discharge flow from the power turbine and converts remaining kinetic energy to static pressure. This diffuser minimizes exhaust losses and improves overall engine efficiency.
shortDescription: Turbine exhaust diffuser/nozzle for Trent 60, reduces exit losses.
technicalDescription: Stainless steel exhaust diffuser with aerodynamic struts, expansion joints, and flange connections. Designed to maximize static pressure recovery with minimal total pressure loss. Includes thermocouple and pressure tap instrumentation ports.
features: Aerodynamic strut design for minimum pressure loss
Expansion joints for thermal stress relief
benefits: Increases overall cycle efficiency by reducing exhaust losses
Smooth transition to exhaust ducting or HRSG
Long service life with corrosion-resistant materials
dimensions: 1,800mm inlet OD x 2,400mm outlet OD x 1,200mm length
seoDescription: Rolls-Royce Trent exhaust diffuser/nozzle, 304L SS, high pressure recovery. For aero-derivative gas turbine combined cycle and marine applications.

faq.question: What is static pressure recovery?
faq.answer: The diffuser converts exhaust velocity energy into static pressure, reducing back pressure on the turbine and improving efficiency.

faq.question: Can this be connected to a HRSG?
faq.answer: Yes, the flanged outlet is designed for direct connection to heat recovery steam generator ducting.

faq.question: What instrumentation is included?
faq.answer: Pressure taps and thermocouple ports for exhaust temperature and pressure monitoring.

name: Siemens SGT-400 Carbon Shaft Seal Assembly
description: The Siemens SGT-400 carbon shaft seal assembly prevents bearing oil leakage along the turbine shaft. The segmented carbon ring design provides effective sealing with low friction at high shaft speeds.
shortDescription: Segmented carbon shaft seal assembly for SGT-400, oil-side sealing.
technicalDescription: Segmented carbon ring seal with garter spring loading, integral oil slinger, and labyrinth gas-side seal. Designed for oil-to-atmosphere sealing at turbine shaft penetrations. Carbon grade selected for low wear at high surface speeds.
features: Segmented carbon ring for radial compliance
Integral oil slinger prevents oil migration
Low friction carbon material for high-speed operation
benefits: Prevents oil leakage into turbine compartment
Reduces oil consumption and environmental impact
Long service life with self-lubricating carbon material
seoTitle: Siemens SGT-400 Carbon Shaft Seal Assembly
seoDescription: Siemens SGT-400 segmented carbon shaft seal assembly for turbine bearing oil sealing. Low friction, high speed, prevents oil leakage.

faq.question: What is the expected seal life?
faq.answer: Carbon seal life typically exceeds 24,000 operating hours before replacement.

faq.question: Can the carbon rings be replaced individually?
faq.answer: Yes, individual carbon segments are replaceable, reducing replacement cost.

faq.question: What causes carbon seal wear?
faq.answer: Normal wear is from start/stop cycles. Abnormal wear may indicate shaft vibration or oil contamination.
